sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Uber and Trial Lawyers Spar Over New Yorkers’ Auto Insurance Premiums
feed.summary: Uber and the New York State Trial Lawyers Association are clashing over a proposed bill in New York that aims to lower auto insurance premiums by capping damages. Governor Kathy Hochul supports the bill, which would also limit payouts in cases where motorists are mostly at fault or impaired. While trial lawyers oppose the measure, Uber, a major auto insurance purchaser, is heavily lobbying for its passage, arguing the current system is prone to fraud.

feed.title: Assessing Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) Valuation After Autoimmune Pipeline Advances And New R&D Partnership
feed.summary: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) has seen its share price climb following positive Phase 3 results for its oral psoriasis drug ICOTYDE and a significant R&D partnership with Royalty Pharma for JNJ-4804. Investors are encouraged to review both qualitative and quantitative factors to determine their stance on JNJ's future prospects.

feed.title: The Ministry of Health is interested in a new, still poorly researched drug against radiation damage. The agency sent a request to the developer - the American biotech company Tivic Health Systems. Here's what we learned
feed.summary: The Ministry of Health has contacted Tivic Health Systems, an American biotech company, regarding their drug Entolimod. This drug is being considered for inclusion in Ukraine's strategic reserve as a treatment for acute radiation sickness. Entolimod is specifically designed to combat gastrointestinal damage from high radiation doses, unlike existing treatments, and has received Fast Track and Orphan Drug designations from the US FDA.

feed.title: Toll Brothers, Tri Pointe Homes to Build 1,655-Home Community in Richmond
feed.summary: Toll Brothers Inc. and Tri Pointe Homes are partnering on Evergrove, a new 1,655-home master-planned community in Richmond, Fort Bend County, Texas. This joint venture will develop a 911-acre tract, with each builder constructing approximately 827 homes. Presales are expected to begin in June, with the first move-ins projected for early 2027, offering homes ranging from the $400,000s to over $1 million.
